subject: Mathematics
title: The Surprise Exam Paradox
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Surprise Exam Paradox.

hook.text: A teacher says: 'There will be a surprise exam next week. You won't be able to predict the day.'

buildup.text: Students reason: it can't be Friday, because by Thursday night they'd know. So eliminate Friday.

discovery.text: By the same logic, Thursday is out, then Wednesday, Tuesday, and Monday. No day works.

twist.text: But the teacher gives the exam on Wednesday—and everyone is indeed surprised.

climax.text: The paradox reveals a gap between logical reasoning about knowledge and actual surprise.

punchline.text: Perfect logic predicted no surprise. The surprise still came.

quiz.question: What does the Surprise Exam Paradox challenge?
options: The relationship between logic and knowledge
The length of a school week
